name: Don't Return Null
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 7: Error Handling
problem: Methods that return null force every caller to add a null check, and when even one caller forgets, the result is a NullPointerException at an unrelated location, making the root cause hard to trace.
steps: Return an empty collection instead of null when a list, set, or map result has no entries.
Return a Special Case object (Null Object) instead of null for a missing domain entity.
Return Optional<T> to make the possibility of absence explicit and compiler-enforced.
Throw a meaningful exception if the absence of a value is truly an error condition.
Audit all return statements for null and replace with one of the above strategies.
